result:
**Goal: Add an Embryogenesis & Gastrulation simulation mode.**

Model the development of a multicellular organism from a single fertilized egg through cleavage, blastula formation, gastrulation (invagination), and tissue differentiation — all driven by morphogen gradients, cell-cell signaling, and differential gene expression.

This is the natural sequel to the Primordial Soup mode: origin of life → development of complex organisms. Key features would include:

- **Cleavage divisions** — symmetric then asymmetric mitosis producing a ball of cells (morula → blastula with hollow cavity)
- **Morphogen gradients** — diffusible signals (BMP, Wnt, Nodal analogs) establishing dorsal-ventral and anterior-posterior axes via French Flag positional information
- **Gastrulation** — invagination/epiboly mechanics forming the three germ layers (ectoderm/mesoderm/endoderm) with convergent extension cell movements
- **Cell fate determination** — gene regulatory network state per cell (pluripotent → committed → differentiated) responding to morphogen thresholds and neighbor signals
- **Tissue self-organization** — differential adhesion sorting (Steinberg hypothesis), apical-basal polarity, neural tube folding
- **3 visualization views** — cross-section showing cell types/morphogen overlay, fate map coloring by lineage/germ layer, time-series sparklines for cell count/differentiation/morphogen levels/cavity size/axis polarity
- **6 presets** — Normal Development, Axis Duplication (Spemann organizer transplant), Neural Tube Defect, Morphogen Knockout, Twinning Event, Accelerated Gastrulation

Shall I build it?
